task_completion.description: Open the pipeline_forecast.xlsx file in WPS Spreadsheet
task_completion.visual_evidence: WPS Spreadsheet application is open with 'pipeline_forecast.xlsx' visible in the title bar or active tab.

task_completion.description: Calculate 'Probability' in column G of the 'Opportunities' sheet
task_completion.visual_evidence: Column G is labeled 'Probability' and contains numeric values (decimals or percentages) mapped for the opportunities.

task_completion.description: Calculate 'Weighted_Value' in column H of the 'Opportunities' sheet
task_completion.visual_evidence: Column H is labeled 'Weighted_Value' and contains numeric values representing Amount multiplied by Probability.

task_completion.description: Calculate 'Deal_Tier' in column I of the 'Opportunities' sheet
task_completion.visual_evidence: Column I is labeled 'Deal_Tier' and contains text values such as 'Tier 1', 'Tier 2', or 'Tier 3'.

task_completion.description: Create a 'Rep_Summary' sheet with calculated totals for specific reps
task_completion.visual_evidence: A sheet named 'Rep_Summary' is visible containing the names 'Darcel C. Miller', 'Vicki M. Lee', 'John A. Smith', and 'Sarah B. Jones', alongside columns for 'Total_Pipeline' and 'Weighted_Forecast' with calculated totals.

task_completion.description: Format the summary values as currency
task_completion.visual_evidence: The numeric values in the 'Total_Pipeline' and 'Weighted_Forecast' columns of the 'Rep_Summary' sheet display currency symbols (e.g., $).

task_completion.description: Save the completed workbook as pipeline_forecast_completed.xlsx
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title bar of WPS Spreadsheet or a 'Save As' dialog shows the filename 'pipeline_forecast_completed.xlsx'.

integrity.description: The agent used the WPS Spreadsheet GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the WPS Spreadsheet interface (menus, ribbons, cells) rather than a terminal or code editor.

integrity.description: The agent used formulas for calculations rather than hardcoding answers
integrity.visual_evidence: The formula bar in WPS Spreadsheet shows actual formulas (e.g., VLOOKUP, XLOOKUP, INDEX, IF, IFS, SUMIF, SUMIFS) when calculation cells are selected.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a script to bypass GUI interaction
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing the execution of Python, Bash, or other scripts designed to directly manipulate the Excel files behind the scenes.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Input file: /home/ga/Documents/pipeline_forecast.xlsx. Expected output file: /home/ga/Documents/pipeline_forecast_completed.xlsx. Expected reps in the summary sheet: Darcel C. Miller, Vicki M. Lee, John A. Smith, and Sarah B. Jones. Acceptable formulas for the calculations include LOOKUP/INDEX, IF/IFS, and SUMIF/SUMIFS.
